name: OpenAI
description: AI safety company founded in 2015, creators of GPT-4 and ChatGPT, headquartered in San Francisco

name: Sam Altman
description: CEO of OpenAI, co-founder who leads the company and its mission toward AGI

name: Greg Brockman
description: President and co-founder of OpenAI

name: Ilya Sutskever
description: Co-founder and former Chief Scientist of OpenAI, left in 2024 to start Safe Superintelligence Inc.

name: Elon Musk
description: Co-founded OpenAI in 2015 and later founded xAI; CEO of Tesla and SpaceX

name: San Francisco
description: City in California where OpenAI, Anthropic, and xAI are headquartered

name: GPT-4
description: Large multimodal model by OpenAI released March 2023, accepts image and text inputs, showing human-level performance on benchmarks

name: ChatGPT
description: Conversational AI by OpenAI released November 2022; fastest-growing consumer app reaching 100M users in two months

name: Microsoft
description: Global technology company that invested $10 billion in OpenAI and integrated AI into Azure, Bing, and Microsoft 365

name: Satya Nadella
description: CEO of Microsoft who described the OpenAI partnership as transformative for productivity software

name: Azure OpenAI Service
description: Microsoft's cloud service integrating OpenAI models into Azure for enterprise customers

name: Microsoft 365 Copilot
description: AI-powered productivity assistant integrated into Microsoft 365 using OpenAI technology

name: Anthropic
description: AI safety company founded in 2021, creators of Claude, backed by Google and Amazon with $7.3B raised

name: Dario Amodei
description: CEO and co-founder of Anthropic, previously worked at OpenAI

name: Daniela Amodei
description: President and co-founder of Anthropic

name: Claude 3
description: Anthropic's AI assistant family released March 2024 including Haiku (fastest), Sonnet (balanced), and Opus (most capable)

name: Constitutional AI
description: Anthropic's training methodology to make Claude helpful, harmless, and honest

name: Amazon Web Services
description: Cloud computing division of Amazon that invested $4 billion in Anthropic in 2023

name: Amazon Bedrock
description: AWS managed AI service offering Anthropic's Claude models alongside other foundation models

name: Google DeepMind
description: AI research lab formed April 2023 from merger of Google Brain and DeepMind, headquartered in London

name: Demis Hassabis
description: CEO and co-founder of Google DeepMind

name: Jeff Dean
description: Former head of Google Brain, Chief Scientist at Google

name: AlphaFold
description: Google DeepMind system that solves protein structure prediction with atomic accuracy

name: Gemini
description: Google DeepMind's multimodal AI model family released December 2023; Ultra variant outperformed GPT-4 on MMLU

name: London
description: City in England where Google DeepMind is headquartered

name: Meta AI Research
description: AI research division of Meta Platforms (formerly Facebook)

name: Yann LeCun
description: Chief AI Scientist at Meta, pioneer of convolutional neural networks

name: LLaMA
description: Meta's open-source large language model family; LLaMA 2 (July 2023), Llama 3 (April 2024) rivaling GPT-4

name: xAI
description: AI company founded by Elon Musk in March 2023, creators of Grok, raised $6B in May 2024 at $24B valuation

name: Grok
description: xAI's conversational AI; Grok-1 is a 314B parameter mixture-of-experts model trained on X/Twitter data

name: Mistral AI
description: French AI company founded April 2023, creators of Mixtral, raised $415M Series A, backed by a16z and Lightspeed

name: Arthur Mensch
description: Co-founder and CEO of Mistral AI, formerly at DeepMind

name: Mixtral 8x7B
description: Mistral AI's sparse mixture-of-experts model (December 2023) outperforming LLaMA 2 70B on benchmarks

name: Paris
description: French capital city where Mistral AI is headquartered

name: Transformer
description: Architecture from 2017 'Attention Is All You Need' paper by Google Brain; replaced RNNs with self-attention for faster parallel training

name: Google Brain
description: Google's AI research team that developed the Transformer architecture, later merged with DeepMind

name: BERT
description: Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers by Google; pre-trained with masked language modeling, used in Google Search since 2019

name: Jacob Devlin
description: Lead researcher on BERT at Google AI Language

name: GPT-3
description: OpenAI's GPT-3 (175B parameters, 2020) demonstrated remarkable few-shot learning; GPT-2 was 1.5B params (2019)

name: Alec Radford
description: Lead researcher on GPT papers at OpenAI

name: RAG
description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation from Facebook AI Research (2020) combining parametric LM memory with non-parametric document retrieval

name: Facebook AI Research
description: Meta's AI research lab that developed RAG, FAISS, and LLaMA

name: GraphRAG
description: Microsoft Research system that builds a knowledge graph from documents (entity extraction + community detection) then retrieves via graph traversal and vector search

name: Microsoft Research
description: Research division of Microsoft that developed the GraphRAG system

name: Darren Edge
description: Principal author of the GraphRAG paper at Microsoft Research

name: FAISS
description: Facebook AI Similarity Search library (2017) for efficient billion-scale dense vector search supporting HNSW, IVF, Flat indexes

name: Leiden Algorithm
description: Community detection algorithm (2019, Traag et al.) guaranteeing well-connected communities; improves over Louvain; used in GraphRAG

name: LangChain
description: Open-source LLM framework by Harrison Chase (Oct 2022) for building chains, RAG pipelines, and agents; 80K+ GitHub stars

name: Harrison Chase
description: Creator of LangChain; raised $25M seed round from Benchmark in April 2023

name: LlamaIndex
description: Data framework by Jerry Liu (Nov 2022) for connecting custom data sources to LLMs with hundreds of data connectors

name: Jerry Liu
description: Creator of LlamaIndex; raised $8.5M seed round in 2023

name: Hugging Face
description: ML tools company (founded 2016) hosting 500K+ open-source models; raised $235M Series D at $4.5B valuation

name: Transformers Library
description: Hugging Face's Python library with 100K+ pre-trained NLP models; the most widely-used NLP library

name: Clement Delangue
description: Co-founder and CEO of Hugging Face
